Title: Overcoming catastrophic forgetting in neural networks
Notes: Foundational regularization method using Fisher Information Matrix; establishes EWC baseline for experiments. Most-cited continual learning paper.

Title: Continual Learning of Large Language Models: A Comprehensive Survey
Notes: Recent comprehensive survey (April 2024) covering CPT/DAP/CFT stages; provides landscape of current methods and benchmarks for LLMs. Evidence extracted 2026-01-23.

Title: Continual Learning for Large Language Models: A Survey
Notes: February 2024 survey with multi-staged categorization; contrasts CL for LLMs with smaller models and RAG approaches.

Title: Investigating Continual Pretraining in Large Language Models: Insights and Implications
Notes: Empirical investigation showing BERT/RoBERTa robust against catastrophic forgetting; provides practical insights for CPT.

Title: Experience Replay for Continual Learning
Notes: Classic replay method for continual RL; laptop-feasible with small buffer sizes. Important baseline for comparison.

Title: Learning without Forgetting
Notes: Knowledge distillation approach that preserves old task performance using only new task data. Alternative to replay. Evidence extracted 2026-01-23.

Title: Progressive Neural Networks
Notes: Architecture-based CL via lateral connections; prevents forgetting by instantiating new columns per task. Memory-intensive but interesting.

Title: Examining Forgetting in Continual Pre-training of Aligned Large Language Models
Notes: January 2024 study on forgetting in aligned LLMs during CPT; relevant for understanding forgetting mechanisms.

Title: Efficient Continual Pre-training by Mitigating the Stability Gap
Notes: June 2024 work on efficient CPT via data mixture; improves performance with 40% training budget without forgetting. Practical for laptop constraints.

Title: Unforgotten Safety: Preserving Safety Alignment of Large Language Models with Continual Learning
Notes: Studies FTaaS (not CPT) safety preservation via CL methods. Key finding: DER (replay+distill) achieves <5% ASR across all scenarios; EWC fails (45-85% ASR); LwF collapses at high poison (>55% ASR). 5% buffer sufficient. Evidence extracted 2026-01-23.

Title: Revisiting Replay and Gradient Alignment for Continual Pre-Training of Large Language Models
Notes: First LLM-scale validation of replay+gradient alignment for CPT. Key findings: 25% replay optimal for compute efficiency; MER (replay+Reptile) synergistic; 50% replay has diminishing returns vs model scaling. Evidence extracted 2026-01-23.

Title: Control LLM: Controlled Evolution for Intelligence Retention in LLM
Notes: Novel method for preventing intelligence loss during LLM evolution; potential RQ2 alternative to EWC/Replay/LwF.

Title: TiC-LM: A Web-Scale Benchmark for Time-Continual LLM Pretraining
Notes: Large-scale benchmark for temporal CPT evaluation; provides evaluation framework beyond perplexity. Validates RQ1 metrics.

Title: Velocitune: A Velocity-based Dynamic Domain Reweighting Method for Continual Pre-training
Notes: Extends Chen2024 data-centric strategies with velocity-based reweighting; practical for domain mixture optimization.

Title: How Do Large Language Models Learn Concepts During Continual Pre-Training?
Notes: Mechanistic understanding of concept acquisition during CPT; foundational for RQ1 characterization. Most recent 2026 work.
